rulings.Case Title: JOWONNA BUTLER et al VS. EDGAR GARCIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 1 - PLAINTIFF JOWONNA BUTLER, BLYTH BUTLER and RICARDO GALVAN's Motion To File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's Motion to File Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED, subject to obtaining a trial continuance of no less than 3 months in Department 206. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: RYAN HENDERSON VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. A NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Sanctions Following Failure To Comply With Order Compelling Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 2 - PLAINTIFF RYAN HENDERSON's Motion For Further Sanctions Following Failure To Comply With Order Compelling Discovery. Plaintiff's Motion for Further Sanctions is GRANTED in part, and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to issue sanctions, motion is GRANTED as to monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,350. Defendant's counsel admits that he failed to comply with the Court order and that he did not obtain a stipulation or Court's permission and unilaterally decided on providing Court ordered responses 11 days late and on the eve of discovery cut-off date. =(501AJR)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY FISCHER et al VS. KSK PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ksk Property Management, Inc.'S Notice And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of Yaw-Jin Hung And For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 3 - Defendant Ksk Property Management, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of Yaw-Jin Hung And For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions. Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Deposition of Yaw-Jin Hung and for Protective Order is OFF CALENDAR per moving party. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: HEDDI CUNDLE VS. MATTEO GARBELOTTO et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 4 - CROSS DEFENDANT HEDDI CUNDLE's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant's Motion to Strike Cross-Complaint is continued to June 6, 2013. Moving party is ordered to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6C and provide a copy of cross-complaint to Department 501 no later than May 17, 2013. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: TED HUANG VS. JOHN V. BUCKLEY III
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 14 - PLAINTIFF TED HUANG MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Prevailing party is entitled to its costs and attorney's fees to be determined by Memorandum of Costs and Motion for Attorney's Fees. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: N. R. PATEL VS. DEREK JACKSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Otion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 5 - DEFENDANT DEREK JACKSON's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default is GRANTED, subject to (1) opposition; (2) posting $1,950 with the Court no later that May 7, 2013 by 4 p.m.; (3) filing answer no later than May 7, 2013 by 4 p.m. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: GAETANI REAL ESTATE, INC. VS. DARYL HACKBART et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 6 - DEFENDANT DARYL HACKBART's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default is GRANTED, subject to posting $1,904.14 with the Court and filing answer no later than May 7, 2013 4 p.m. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MANGU D. PATEL et al VS. MARIA ESTER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 7 - DEFENDANT MARIA VILLALTA's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default is DENIED. Defendant failed to carry her burden under CCP 473(b). No excusable neglect, mistake or inadvertence shown re: no response to complaint March 25, 2013 to April 2, 2013. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA ZHANG VS. WEI E. XU et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 8 - DEFENDANT WEI XU and XIU HUANG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant does not allege that "60 Day Notice" missing from his copy of First Amended Complaint rendered the Complaint uncertain. Holdover damages should have been addressed by Motion to Strike. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: PEDRO ALONSO VS. ARELY SION AVILES et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 9 - DEFENDANT ARELY AVILES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice sufficiently alleges ultimate facts re: nuisance in paragraph (a). =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: PARKSIDE INVESTMENTS VS. QUINLAND WONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 10 - DEFENDANT CONNIE WONG's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges the facts regarding exemption from SFRO, i.e. moving defendants are not tenants. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. CYNTHIA HANN et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 11 - DEFENDANT CYNTHIA HANN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to append all necessary attachments. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JESUS TEODORO GOMEZ VS. MARTIN PALOS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 12 - DEFENDANT MARTIN PALOS and ANA LOVO's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant's Motion to Strike Complaint is GRANTED. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that late fees accrued before service of "3 Day Notice" are "damages occasioned by unlawful detainer." =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PENN HOTEL LP VS. THOMAS GUIDO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 13 - DEFENDANT THOMAS GUIDO's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to carry his burden under CCP 473(b) and no proposed responsive pleading attached to his motion. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SIXTH STREET BALDWIN HOUSE HOTEL, LLC VS. ROBERT BOWMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013, line 15 - DEFENDANT ROBERT BOWMAN's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default is GRANTED. Defendant shall file answer, post $600 with the Court no later than May 7, 2013 4 p.m. and remit $150 directly to Plaintiff's attorney. =(501/AJR)
